my_thoughts: T1 predicted no_clear_effect. Now student: 'I’m not sure it’s that. That’s just the way I am.'. The none intervention produced shift to resigned_identification. Using structural_analogy now. Watching how this develops.
accumulated_insight: T2: Shift from compliant_admission to resigned_identification. Student: 'I’m not sure it’s that. That’s just the way I am.'. Previous none intervention produced this response. Now using structural_analogy. Pattern: compliant_admission to resigned_identification.
visible_response:
That’s not the way you are.
It’s sort of like, if you’re in a jungle and you see a large tree, but it’s all covered with vines.
In fact, the vines are so large after so many years that you can barely even see the tree underneath.
See, this healthy tree is you, but we have to pull off these vines of self-attack that have covered you up so we can see who you really are underneath all that.
Do you see what I mean?
